rag_rules: Keep type-level contract content with the component type owner, and keep implementation-only binding content with the implementation owner.
Insert implementation-scoped annex content only after the structural declarations it depends on, without changing the surrounding architecture.
generic_rules: Match annex ownership to symbol visibility: type owners may reference only type-visible features, while implementation owners may reference implementation paths and bindings.
Preserve all existing features, subcomponents, connections, and properties when adding an annex block.
If generated content refers to unknown or nonvisible identifiers, do not invent new AADL declarations during fusion.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use the standard `annex agree {** ... **};` wrapper inside the chosen type or implementation body.
Implementation-scoped binding statements belong in implementation scope, not in a type-level annex.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only paths or subcomponent ports into a component type annex.
Do not move, rename, or rewrite architecture elements just to accommodate annex insertion.
irrelevant_topics: Timing or property names used as AGREE expression variables
